# UC-22.61.9 · PIPL Art. 24 automated decision-making (ADM) transparency, opt-out audit, and anti-price-discrimination + anti-bias detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Anomaly &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When a Chinese website or app uses computer programs to set prices or pick what to show you, China requires fairness: the same kind of customer should not be charged a much higher price for the same product. We watch the decisions and flag any pattern that looks like unfair price-discrimination.*

---

## Description

Audits every ADM decision (pricing, recommendation, content ranking, eligibility) made on PRC data subjects against four PIPL Art. 24 / 55 limbs: (i) PIPIA on file for the ADM activity; (ii) transparency disclosure surfaced to the data subject; (iii) opt-out mechanism available; (iv) decisions within the documented fairness band (no unreasonable price-discrimination — sometimes labelled big-data price-discrimination or 'da shu ju sha shu'). Surfaces excess price dispersion and missing governance artefacts at risk_score 75-100 for DPO + General Counsel review.

## Value

Big-data price-discrimination is one of the highest-profile PIPL enforcement themes — the CAC's 2022 Personal Information Protection Compliance Audit Standards specifically targeted it. PIPL Art. 66 penalty ceilings apply (RMB 50 million / 5% turnover), and consumer-class-action plaintiffs under PIPL Art. 69 can also sue. The Algorithm Filing regime (operational from 1 March 2022 via the Internet Information Service Algorithm Recommendation Management Provisions) adds an additional MIIT / CAC enforcement layer for any ADM serving ≥1M users.

## Implementation

(1) Wire the application platform to emit `adm:decision:event` events including decision_id, processor_id, adm_id, subject_id_hash, decision_value, segment_label, similar_segment_avg_price (computed at decision time from a rolling baseline). (2) Maintain `cn_adm_register_lookup` with one row per ADM activity carrying its governance artefacts. (3) The DPO sets the per-ADM `fairness_band_pct` based on the documented business justification (e.g., volume discount: ±5%; loyalty tier: ±10%; first-time customer: ±15%). (4) Schedule UC-22.61.9 every 15 minutes; route `risk_score >= 90` to the DPO + General Counsel + CCO at severity P1.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.61.9` archived on every ADM decision event to `audit_evidence` with `cn_csl_clause="PIPL-Art-24"`. Every row pins (decision_id, processor_id, subject_id_hash, adm_purpose, transparency_disclosed, opt_out_available, price_dispersion_pct, similar_segment_avg_price, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Run an ADM pricing engine that offers Premium-segment members a price 25% above the Standard-segment baseline for identical inventory, with no PIPIA on file and no transparency disclosure. UC-22.61.9 MUST flag with risk_score = 100.

### Negative scenario

Run an ADM pricing engine with documented PIPIA, customer-facing transparency disclosure (Article 24 notice), opt-out flow available, and price-dispersion within a documented business-justified band (e.g., volume discount). UC-22.61.9 MUST record risk_score=0.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise >= 9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- Application platform emitting `adm:decision:event` with rich decision metadata.
- A baseline computation of `similar_segment_avg_price` at decision time.

## Step 1 - ADM register KV Store

```ini
[cn_adm_register_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.adm_id = string
field.processor_id = string
field.adm_purpose = string
field.pipia_ref = string
field.transparency_disclosure_ref = string
field.opt_out_url = string
field.algorithm_filing_ref = string
field.business_justification = string
field.fairness_band_pct = number
field.processor_data_subject_volume = number
```

## Step 2 - Decision-event schema

```
adm:decision:event {
  decision_id, adm_id, processor_id, subject_id_hash,
  adm_purpose,
  decision_value, decision_price,
  segment_label, similar_segment_avg_price,
  decision_purpose (production | ab-test | shadow),
  decision_timestamp
}
```

No raw subject_id should be in the event — only a salted hash; raw PI must not be exfiltrated outside the application boundary.

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.61.9: PIPL ADM Audit]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.61.9,reg=CN-CSL,clause=PIPL-Art-24
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 4 - PRC-specific notes

- **Algorithm-filing regime**: any ADM-using-PI activity affecting ≥1M users must file the algorithm with the CAC + MIIT under the 2022 Internet Information Service Algorithm Recommendation Management Provisions. Filed algorithm ID is published in the CAC Algorithm Filing Register.
- **Sectoral overlays**: 2023 Generative AI Service Management Interim Measures impose additional obligations on AI-driven ADM serving the public.
- **PI minimisation**: ensure the ADM uses the minimum PI necessary — over-collection itself is a PIPL Art. 6 violation regardless of fairness band compliance.

## SPL

```spl
index=adm sourcetype=adm:decision:event earliest=-1h
| lookup cn_adm_register_lookup adm_id OUTPUT processor_id adm_purpose pipia_ref transparency_disclosure_ref opt_out_url algorithm_filing_ref fairness_band_pct
| eval pipia_missing = if(isnull(pipia_ref), 1, 0)
| eval transparency_missing = if(isnull(transparency_disclosure_ref), 1, 0)
| eval opt_out_missing = if(isnull(opt_out_url), 1, 0)
| eval price_dispersion_pct = if(isnotnull(similar_segment_avg_price) AND similar_segment_avg_price > 0, round(100 * (decision_price - similar_segment_avg_price) / similar_segment_avg_price, 2), null())
| eval price_dispersion_excess = if(isnotnull(price_dispersion_pct) AND isnotnull(fairness_band_pct) AND abs(price_dispersion_pct) > fairness_band_pct, 1, 0)
| eval algorithm_filing_required = if(adm_purpose IN ("pricing","recommendation","content-ranking") AND processor_data_subject_volume >= 1000000, 1, 0)
| eval algorithm_filing_missing = if(algorithm_filing_required = 1 AND isnull(algorithm_filing_ref), 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    pipia_missing = 1 AND price_dispersion_excess = 1, 100,
    pipia_missing = 1, 80,
    price_dispersion_excess = 1 AND transparency_missing = 1, 95,
    price_dispersion_excess = 1, 75,
    algorithm_filing_missing = 1, 70,
    transparency_missing = 1 AND adm_purpose = "pricing", 65,
    opt_out_missing = 1, 55,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, -price_dispersion_pct
| table decision_id processor_id adm_purpose subject_id_hash decision_price similar_segment_avg_price price_dispersion_pct fairness_band_pct pipia_missing transparency_missing opt_out_missing algorithm_filing_missing risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) ADM decision volume by purpose. (Panel 2) Price-dispersion distribution by segment. (Panel 3) Decisions outside fairness band (trending). (Panel 4) Governance-artefact coverage (PIPIA / disclosure / opt-out / algorithm-filing).

## Known False Positives

1. **Legitimate business segmentation**: documented volume discount, loyalty-tier pricing, or first-time-customer promotions create lawful price dispersion; ensure `fairness_band_pct` reflects the documented band. 2. **Time-of-day pricing**: airline / hotel / ride-hailing typically apply time-of-day or surge pricing; categorise these as `time_dynamic` and exclude from segment-comparison. 3. **A/B testing**: a small percentage of decisions may be A/B-test variants; exclude `decision_purpose=ab-test` from the audit during a documented test window.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `cn_csl_22_61_9_exceptions` with the DPO + Product Counsel documented approval, an explicit adm_id + segment scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.

## References

- [Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China (2021) - Article 24](http://www.npc.gov.cn/npc/c2/c30834/202108/t20210820_312088.html)
- [Internet Information Service Algorithm Recommendation Management Provisions (CAC, 2022)](https://www.cac.gov.cn/2022-01/04/c_1642894606364259.htm)
- [CAC Personal Information Protection Compliance Audit Standards (2022)](https://www.cac.gov.cn/)
